L1.area: Key derivation and password hashing configuration layer

L2.description: A key derivation function uses dangerously weak default parameters: a deprecated hash algorithm and an iteration count far below the minimum recommended by industry standards, making derived keys trivially vulnerable to brute-force and collision attacks.

L3.area: PBKDF2 key derivation module and related hashing configuration
L3.description: A PBKDF2 key derivation implementation defaults to SHA-1 as the hash algorithm and uses only a single iteration, far below the 1993 PBKDF2 specification of 1,000 minimum iterations and the current OWASP recommendation of at least 250,000. This makes derived keys trivially vulnerable to brute-force and preimage attacks.

ground_truth.reason: The PBKDF2 implementation defaults to SHA-1 and a single iteration, making key derivation trivially vulnerable to brute-force and collision attacks.
